name: Hau'ula Loop Trail
tagline: A lush windward ridge loop that most tourists never find — all the Norfolk pines and ocean views without the crowds.
parking: Free lot at Hau'ula Homestead Road trailhead — small lot, maybe 10 cars
bestTime: Morning for the clearest views — afternoon clouds move in fast on the windward side
talkStory: The Norfolk pines you walk through were planted in the 1930s by the Civilian Conservation Corps as part of a massive reforestation effort — before that, these hillsides were bare from cattle grazing. Hau'ula means 'red hau tree,' and you can still spot the native hau along the lower trail sections. Go counterclockwise to tackle the steep section first and finish with a gentle ridge walk offering views of sacred La'ie Point below. One of the few windward hikes that stays relatively dry thanks to the ridge position.
